_metadata.title: Transparency Obligations for Certain AI Systems

published: C2PA Technical Specification v2.1 — Content provenance and authenticity
IPTC Photo Metadata Standard 2024.1 — DigitalSourceType for AI-generated media
ISO/IEC 12792:2024 — Transparency taxonomy of AI systems
NIST AI 100-4 — Reducing Risks Posed by Synthetic Content
upcoming: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 50 (expected Q4 2026)

primary_official_source.name: EUR-Lex — Official Journal of the European Union
primary_official_source.eli_note: ELI (European Legislation Identifier) is designed for permanent persistence. Preferred over CELEX-based URLs.

obligations.source_quote: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use.
obligations.addressee_note: Provider of an AI system intended to interact directly with natural persons (e.g., chatbots, virtual assistants, AI customer service). NOT limited to high-risk systems.

decomposed_atoms.description: The AI system must inform natural persons that they are interacting with an AI system
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The AI system SHALL inform natural persons that they are interacting with an AI system — the disclosure must be proactive, not requiring the user to discover this fact

decomposed_atoms.description: The system must be designed and developed with transparency as a built-in feature
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The system SHALL be designed and developed in such a way that the AI disclosure is part of the system's architecture — not an afterthought or optional feature

decomposed_atoms.description: The disclosure must be clear and distinguishable to the natural person
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The AI interaction disclosure SHALL be presented clearly and in a distinguishable manner — not buried in terms of service, not in fine print, not easily missed

exceptions.description: Disclosure is not required when it is 'obvious from the circumstances and context of use' that the person is interacting with an AI system
exceptions.burden_of_proof: The deployer bears the burden of demonstrating that AI interaction is obvious from context
exceptions.note: This exception should be interpreted narrowly — most conversational AI interactions are NOT obviously AI-driven

cross_references.context: Information under Art. 50(1) must be provided in a clear, distinguishable manner by the time of first interaction

obligations.source_quote: Providers of AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems, generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content, shall ensure that the outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated.
obligations.addressee_note: Provider of any AI system (including GPAI) that generates synthetic content. Covers image generators, TTS, video generators, and text generation systems.

decomposed_atoms.description: AI-generated content must be marked in a machine-readable format
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The outputs of the AI system SHALL be marked in a machine-readable format — human-visible watermarks alone are NOT sufficient; the marking must be detectable by automated systems

decomposed_atoms.description: The marking must make the content detectable as artificially generated or manipulated
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The marking SHALL make the content detectable as artificially generated or manipulated — enabling downstream systems and users to verify the origin of content

decomposed_atoms.description: Marking must apply to all content modalities the system generates
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Marking SHALL cover ALL output modalities: synthetic audio, image, video, AND text — if the system generates multiple types, each must be marked

decomposed_atoms.description: Technical marking must be interoperable, robust against manipulation, and reliable
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The marking technique SHALL be interoperable (works across platforms), robust (resistant to removal or alteration), and reliable (consistently applied and detectable)

recommended_standards.description: Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity — cryptographically signed content credentials embedded in media files
recommended_standards.eu_alignment: Referenced by EU AI Office guidelines as suitable technical standard for Art. 50(2)

recommended_standards.description: DigitalSourceType: trainedAlgorithmicMedia — standardized metadata field for AI-generated images
recommended_standards.eu_alignment: Widely adopted in media industry; interoperable with C2PA

recommended_standards.description: Google DeepMind imperceptible watermarking for AI-generated images and text
recommended_standards.eu_alignment: Cited in NIST AI 100-4 as an example of robust content marking

cross_references.context: Deep fake disclosure obligation (Art. 50(4)) extends marking requirement for manipulated content depicting real persons
Information must be provided in a clear, distinguishable manner

obligations.source_quote: Deployers of an emotion recognition system or a biometric categorisation system shall inform the natural persons exposed thereto of the operation of the system, and shall process the personal data in accordance with Regulations (EU) 2016/679 and (EU) 2018/1725 and Directive (EU) 2016/680, as applicable.
obligations.addressee_note: DEPLOYER (not provider) of emotion recognition or biometric categorization systems. This is a deployment-time obligation, reflecting that the deployer controls where and how the system is used.

decomposed_atoms.description: Natural persons exposed to the system must be informed of its operation
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The deployer SHALL inform all natural persons exposed to the emotion recognition or biometric categorization system of its operation — this includes persons in physical spaces (signage) and digital users (in-app notification)

decomposed_atoms.description: The information must describe the operation of the system
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The deployer SHALL explain what the system does — e.g., that it detects emotions from facial expressions, or categorizes persons by age/gender from biometric data

decomposed_atoms.description: Personal data must be processed in accordance with GDPR/LED
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The deployer SHALL process personal data in accordance with GDPR (Regulation 2016/679), Regulation 2018/1725 (EU institutions), or the Law Enforcement Directive (2016/680) as applicable

decomposed_atoms.description: GDPR information obligations (Art. 13/14) must be fulfilled
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The deployer SHALL provide information required under GDPR Art. 13 (data collected from data subject) or Art. 14 (data obtained indirectly) — including purpose, legal basis, retention period, and data subject rights

cross_references.context: Data subject information rights and DPIA requirements for high-risk processing
Information rights under the Law Enforcement Directive

obligations.source_quote: Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated.
obligations.addressee_note: DEPLOYER (not provider) of AI systems that generate or manipulate deep fake content. Includes face swapping, voice cloning, lip syncing, and neural rendering of real persons.

decomposed_atoms.description: The deployer must disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The deployer SHALL disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated — this is a mandatory disclosure, not optional

decomposed_atoms.description: Disclosure must accompany the content when published or distributed
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The disclosure SHALL accompany the deep fake content in its distribution — it must travel with the content, not be available only at the point of creation

decomposed_atoms.description: Disclosure must be understandable to the intended audience
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The disclosure SHALL be presented in a manner that is clear and understandable to the audience encountering the content

exceptions.description: Content used for manifestly artistic, creative, satirical, or fictional analogous purposes
exceptions.note: Even excepted content should indicate AI generation 'in an appropriate manner that does not hamper the display or enjoyment of the work'

exceptions.description: Content used for law enforcement purposes where disclosure would undermine the purpose
exceptions.note: Subject to Art. 50(6) exemptions and appropriate safeguards

cross_references.context: Definition of 'deep fake' — AI-generated or manipulated image, audio, or video content that resembles existing persons, objects, places, or events and would falsely appear to be authentic
Art. 50(2) machine-readable marking requirement applies in addition to Art. 50(4) disclosure

obligations.source_quote: Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates text which is published with the purpose of informing the public on matters of public interest shall disclose that the text has been artificially generated or manipulated.
obligations.addressee_note: DEPLOYER (not provider) of AI systems that generate or manipulate text published for public interest purposes. Distinct from OBL-4 which covers image/audio/video deep fakes.
obligations.note: Distinct from OBL-4: OBL-4 covers image/audio/video deep fake disclosure. This obligation covers AI-generated or manipulated TEXT published with the purpose of informing the public on matters of public interest (e.g., news, political commentary, public reports).

decomposed_atoms.description: Disclosure that text was artificially generated or manipulated
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The deployer SHALL disclose that the text has been artificially generated or manipulated

decomposed_atoms.description: Applies when text is published for informing the public on matters of public interest
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The disclosure obligation SHALL apply where text is published with the purpose of informing the public on matters of public interest

exceptions.description: Content used for manifestly artistic, creative, satirical, or fictional analogous purposes
exceptions.note: Even excepted content should indicate AI generation in an appropriate manner

obligations.source_quote: The information referred to in paragraphs 1 to 4 shall be provided to the natural persons concerned in a clear and distinguishable manner at the latest at the time of the first interaction or exposure.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies to both providers (Art. 50(1)-(2)) and deployers (Art. 50(3)-(4)) — whoever is responsible for the respective paragraph obligation.

decomposed_atoms.description: Information must be presented clearly
decomposed_atoms.requirement: All transparency information under Art. 50(1)-(4) SHALL be provided clearly — using plain language, appropriate to the audience, avoiding technical jargon

decomposed_atoms.description: Information must be distinguishable from other content
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Transparency information SHALL be distinguishable — visually or audibly distinct from surrounding content, not blended in or hidden

decomposed_atoms.description: Information must be provided by the time of first interaction at the latest
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Transparency information SHALL be provided at the latest at the time of the first interaction or exposure — not after the fact, not upon request, but proactively before or at the moment of engagement

decomposed_atoms.description: Information must conform to applicable accessibility requirements
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Transparency information SHALL conform to applicable accessibility requirements — ensuring persons with disabilities can also receive the disclosure

cross_references.context: Art. 50(5) sets the presentation and timing standard for ALL transparency obligations in Art. 50

obligations.source_quote: Paragraphs 1 to 4 shall not affect the requirements and obligations set out in Chapter III, and shall be without prejudice to other transparency obligations laid down in Union or national law for deployers of AI systems.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies to all addressees of Art. 50. Ensures Art. 50 obligations do not diminish or replace other transparency obligations.

decomposed_atoms.description: High-risk AI system obligations (Title III) are not affected by Art. 50
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Art. 50 transparency obligations SHALL NOT diminish or replace the requirements and obligations for high-risk AI systems under Title III — both sets of obligations apply cumulatively where applicable

decomposed_atoms.description: Other transparency obligations in EU or national law remain in force
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Art. 50 SHALL be without prejudice to other transparency obligations under Union or national law — including GDPR transparency requirements, consumer protection law, and sector-specific obligations

cross_references.title: Title III
cross_references.context: High-risk AI system requirements (Art. 6-51) — apply in addition to Art. 50 where system is also high-risk

obligations.source_quote: The AI Office shall encourage and facilitate the drawing up of codes of practice at Union level to facilitate the effective implementation of the obligations regarding the detection and labelling of artificially generated or manipulated content.
obligations.addressee_note: The AI Office (not providers or deployers). The AI Office is tasked with encouraging and facilitating industry codes of practice for detection and labelling of AI-generated content.

decomposed_atoms.description: AI Office must encourage development of codes of practice at Union level
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The AI Office SHALL encourage and facilitate the drawing up of codes of practice at Union level

decomposed_atoms.description: Codes of practice must target detection and labelling of artificially generated/manipulated content
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Codes of practice SHALL facilitate effective implementation of obligations regarding detection and labelling of artificially generated or manipulated content

cross_references.context: AI Office codes of practice target effective implementation of the Art. 50(2) marking obligation

obligations.source_quote: The Commission may adopt implementing acts to establish the technical specifications for the machine-readable format referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article.
obligations.note: Commission empowerment to specify the technical format for machine-readable content marking (Art. 50(2)). Providers should monitor for implementing acts that may specify required formats.

decomposed_atoms.description: Commission may adopt implementing acts for machine-readable format specifications
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The Commission MAY adopt implementing acts to establish technical specifications for the machine-readable format

obligations.source_quote: The Commission may adopt implementing acts to specify and update the list of specific categories of AI systems referred to in paragraph 4 of this Article.
obligations.note: Commission empowerment to specify and update the list of AI system categories subject to Art. 50(4) deep fake disclosure. Providers should monitor for implementing acts that may expand the scope.

decomposed_atoms.description: Commission may specify AI system categories for Art. 50(4) disclosure
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The Commission MAY adopt implementing acts to specify and update the list of AI system categories for Art. 50(4)

scope_notes.broad_scope: Art. 50 applies to ALMOST ALL AI systems — not limited to high-risk. This is the broadest transparency obligation in the EU AI Act.
scope_notes.provider_obligations: ART50-OBL-1 (human interaction disclosure), ART50-OBL-2 (synthetic content marking) — provider must build transparency into the system
scope_notes.deployer_obligations: ART50-OBL-3 (emotion/biometric disclosure), ART50-OBL-4 (deep fake image/audio/video), ART50-OBL-4b (deep fake text for public interest) — deployer must inform persons at deployment time
scope_notes.cross_cutting: ART50-OBL-5 (presentation and timing), ART50-OBL-6 (cumulative obligations) — apply to all Art. 50 obligations
scope_notes.ai_office: ART50-OBL-7 — AI Office SHALL facilitate codes of practice for detection and labelling
scope_notes.commission_empowerments: ART50-EMP-7a (machine-readable format specs), ART50-EMP-7b (system category list) — Commission MAY adopt implementing acts
